codeReview.summary: The ToolDefinition move itself is straightforward, but the feature still fails review because the commit bundles unrelated release-version changes and does not add the JSON-RPC-focused unit coverage required by VAL-TYPES-005.

issues.description: This refactor commit unexpectedly changes the package version from 0.5.5 to 0.5.6 even though the feature is only about consolidating shared JSON-RPC types. Release version bumps should not be bundled into an internal shared-module refactor.
The Chrome extension manifest version is bumped from 0.5.5 to 0.5.6 as part of this shared-type cleanup. That changes shipped extension metadata without being part of the feature requirements.
The Firefox manifest version is also bumped from 0.5.5 to 0.5.6 in this refactor commit. This is another release-scoped side effect unrelated to consolidating json-rpc.ts.

The feature did not add any JSON-RPC-specific unit coverage for `isJsonRpcRequest`, `isJsonRpcNotification`, or `isJsonRpcResponse`, even though VAL-TYPES-005 requires unit-test evidence that these guards still behave identically. `tests/unit/run-unit-tests.ts` registers no JSON-RPC suite, and there is no matching `tests/unit/suites/*json-rpc*` test file for the guards now defined in `packages/shared/src/json-rpc.ts`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker skill recommends `npm run test:unit -- --grep '<relevant-pattern>'`, but this repo's unit runner does not parse grep arguments. Workers can think they executed a scoped regression test when they actually ran the entire suite.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:30-33 recommends `npm run test:unit -- --grep '<relevant-pattern>'`; the reviewed transcript skeleton shows the worker running `npm run test:unit -- --grep 'json-rpc'`; tests/unit/run-unit-tests.ts:37-73 hard-codes suite execution and contains no argv filtering.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture notes are stale after this feature: they still document `shared/src/tools.ts` as the ToolDefinition home and show the old required-field shape instead of the consolidated definition in `json-rpc.ts`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:101-102 still lists `tools.ts`; .factory/library/architecture.md:126-130 shows `ToolDefinition` with required `description`; packages/shared/src/json-rpc.ts:27-32 now defines `ToolDefinition` in `json-rpc.ts` with optional `description` and optional `input_schema`.

summary: Review failed. The shared JSON-RPC consolidation itself is small, but the commit includes unrelated version bumps and still lacks the JSON-RPC unit-test coverage required to substantiate VAL-TYPES-005.
